title: House Approves Bill Ending Record Department of Homeland Security Shutdown
summary: The US House voted Thursday to fund the Department of Homeland Security, ending the longest agency shutdown in history. The bipartisan package excluded immigration enforcement operations and proceeded to President Donald Trump for signature.
contents:
The House of Representatives passed legislation on Thursday, April 30, 2026, to fund the Department of Homeland Security following weeks of delay. The vote broke the deadlock surrounding the longest agency shutdown in the department's history.

The bipartisan package provided funding for much of the Department of Homeland Security operations but notably excluded resources for immigration enforcement functions. This targeted approach reflected compromises made between different political factions during negotiations over the funding measure.

Following House approval, the legislation proceeded to President Donald Trump for presidential signature. The passage of the funding bill marked a significant development in resolving the extended government shutdown that had affected agency operations and personnel.
